post.metaDescription: Introduction to Migrant Student Awards in the USA for STEM Fields As a migrant student in the USA, pursuing a degree in a STEM field can be a challenging y
post.content:
## Introduction to Migrant Student Awards in the USA for STEM Fields
As a migrant student in the USA, pursuing a degree in a STEM field can be a challenging yet rewarding experience. If you are a migrant student looking for financial assistance to support your education in the USA, there are several awards and scholarships available to help you achieve your goals. These awards are specifically designed to recognize and support the academic achievements of migrant students in STEM fields, providing them with the necessary resources to excel in their studies.

## Who Qualifies for Migrant Student Awards
To qualify for migrant student awards in the USA, you must hold a valid visa that allows you to study in the country. The following visa categories are typically eligible for these awards:
* F-1 visa: for academic students
* J-1 visa: for exchange visitors
* H-4 visa: for dependents of H-1B visa holders
* L-2 visa: for dependents of L-1 visa holders
* O-3 visa: for dependents of O-1 visa holders
* TD visa: for dependents of TN visa holders
It is essential to check the specific eligibility criteria for each award, as some may have additional requirements or restrictions.

## Available Scholarships for Migrant Students in STEM Fields
There are numerous scholarships available to support migrant students in STEM fields. Here are five real scholarships that you can consider:
### The Paul and Daisy Soros Fellowships for New Americans
The Paul and Daisy Soros Fellowships for New Americans provides up to $90,000 in funding to support graduate studies in the USA. To be eligible, you must be a new American (a green card holder or naturalized citizen) who is 30 years old or younger. The deadline for application is November 1st, and you can find more information on their official website: https://www.pdsoros.org.
### The National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellowship Program
The National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellowship Program provides up to $138,000 in funding to support graduate studies in STEM fields. To be eligible, you must be a US citizen, national, or permanent resident, and be pursuing a graduate degree in a STEM field. The deadline for application is October 22nd, and you can find more information on their official website: https://www.nsf.gov/funding/pgm_summ.jsp?pims_id=6201.
### The Google Lime Scholarship
The Google Lime Scholarship provides up to $10,000 in funding to support undergraduate or graduate studies in computer science or a related field. To be eligible, you must have a disability and be pursuing a degree in computer science or a related field. The deadline for application is December 6th, and you can find more information on their official website: https://limeconnect.com/opportunities/google-lime-scholarship-program.
### The Microsoft Scholarship Program
The Microsoft Scholarship Program provides up to $20,000 in funding to support undergraduate or graduate studies in computer science or a related field. To be eligible, you must be pursuing a degree in computer science or a related field, and have a minimum GPA of 3.0. The deadline for application is March 31st, and you can find more information on their official website: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/diversity/programs/microsoft-scholarship.
### The Intel Scholarship Program
The Intel Scholarship Program provides up to $10,000 in funding to support undergraduate or graduate studies in STEM fields. To be eligible, you must be pursuing a degree in a STEM field, and have a minimum GPA of 3.0. The deadline for application is May 15th, and you can find more information on their official website: https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/education/intel-scholarship-program.html.

## Application Steps for Migrant Student Awards
To apply for migrant student awards, follow these eight steps:
1. Research the awards: Look for awards that match your eligibility criteria and academic goals.
2. Review the eligibility criteria: Carefully read the eligibility criteria for each award to ensure you qualify.
3. Gather required documents: Collect all necessary documents, including transcripts, letters of recommendation, and personal statements.
4. Fill out the application form: Complete the application form accurately and thoroughly.
5. Submit the application: Submit your application before the deadline.
6. Wait for the review process: Wait for the review committee to assess your application.
7. Receive notification: Receive notification of the outcome of your application.
8. Accept the award: Accept the award and follow any necessary instructions to receive the funding.

## Documents Checklist
To apply for migrant student awards, you will typically need to provide the following documents:
* Transcripts: official transcripts from your current and previous institutions
* Letters of recommendation: letters from academic or professional references
* Personal statement: a statement outlining your academic goals and motivations
* Resume: a current resume or CV
* Proof of visa status: documentation of your valid visa status
* Proof of enrollment: documentation of your enrollment in a US institution

## Frequently Asked Questions
Here are some frequently asked questions about migrant student awards:
Q: What is the deadline for applying for migrant student awards?
A: The deadline for applying for migrant student awards varies depending on the award, but it is typically between October and May. It is essential to check the specific deadline for each award.
Q: Can I apply for multiple awards at the same time?
A: Yes, you can apply for multiple awards at the same time, but be sure to review the eligibility criteria and application requirements for each award carefully.
Q: Do I need to have a high GPA to qualify for migrant student awards?
A: A high GPA can be beneficial, but it is not always a requirement. Some awards prioritize other factors, such as community service or leadership experience.
Q: Can I apply for migrant student awards if I am an international student?
A: Yes, some migrant student awards are available to international students, but you must hold a valid visa that allows you to study in the USA.
Q: How do I know if I am eligible for a migrant student award?
A: To determine your eligibility, carefully review the eligibility criteria for each award and contact the award provider if you have any questions.
Q: Can I apply for migrant student awards if I am a graduate student?
A: Yes, many migrant student awards are available to graduate students, but be sure to review the eligibility criteria and application requirements carefully.
Q: How much funding can I receive from a migrant student award?
A: The amount of funding varies depending on the award, but it can range from $1,000 to $90,000 or more.
Q: Where can I find more information about migrant student awards?
A: You can find more information about migrant student awards on the official websites of the award providers or on websites such as the US Department of Education: https://www.ed.gov and the National Science Foundation: https://www.nsf.gov.

For more information on migrant student awards and other resources, visit https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog.

related.funding: The Fulbright Scholarships cover full tuition, living costs, airfare, and health insurance for the duration of the study in the USA
related.content:
## Introduction to Fulbright Scholarships for Migrant Students
The Fulbright Scholarships are fully funded awards that provide migrant students with the opportunity to pursue postgraduate studies in the USA. These scholarships are designed to promote cultural exchange and academic excellence, and they cover all expenses related to studying in the USA, including tuition fees, living costs, and airfare. Migrant students who are interested in applying for these scholarships can find all the necessary information on this page.

## Eligibility Criteria
To be eligible for the Fulbright Scholarships, migrant students must meet certain criteria. The following visa categories are eligible to apply:
* F-1 visa holders
* F-2 visa holders
* J-1 visa holders
* J-2 visa holders
* Refugee status
* Asylee status
* Temporary Protected Status (TPS)
* Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA)

## Available Scholarships
The following are some of the Fulbright Scholarships available to migrant students in the USA:
### Fulbright Foreign Student Program
The Fulbright Foreign Student Program provides up to $100,000 in funding per year for the duration of the study. To be eligible, applicants must have a bachelor's degree and a minimum GPA of 3.0. The deadline for application is October 2026, and the official website is https://fulbright.americancouncils.org/.
### Fulbright Teaching Assistantship
The Fulbright Teaching Assistantship provides up to $20,000 in funding per year for the duration of the study. To be eligible, applicants must have a bachelor's degree and a minimum GPA of 3.0, and they must be proficient in English. The deadline for application is November 2026, and the official website is https://www.fulbright_teacherexchanges.org/.
### Fulbright Research Grant
The Fulbright Research Grant provides up to $50,000 in funding per year for the duration of the study. To be eligible, applicants must have a master's degree and a minimum GPA of 3.5, and they must have a research proposal. The deadline for application is December 2026, and the official website is https://www.cies.org/.
### Fulbright MBA Scholarship
The Fulbright MBA Scholarship provides up to $100,000 in funding per year for the duration of the study. To be eligible, applicants must have a bachelor's degree and a minimum GPA of 3.0, and they must have a minimum of two years of work experience. The deadline for application is January 2027, and the official website is https://www.usief.org.in/.
### Fulbright Law Scholarship
The Fulbright Law Scholarship provides up to $50,000 in funding per year for the duration of the study. To be eligible, applicants must have a law degree and a minimum GPA of 3.0, and they must have a minimum of two years of work experience. The deadline for application is March 2027, and the official website is https://www.fulbrightonline.org/.

## Application Process
To apply for the Fulbright Scholarships, migrant students must follow these steps:
1. Choose the scholarship program that best fits their needs and interests.
2. Review the eligibility criteria and ensure they meet all the requirements.
3. Prepare all the necessary documents, including transcripts, letters of recommendation, and a personal statement.
4. Fill out the online application form and submit it before the deadline.
5. Take the required standardized tests, such as the TOEFL or IELTS.
6. Prepare for and participate in an interview with the Fulbright committee.
7. Receive and review the admission decision and scholarship award.
8. Accept the scholarship award and prepare to begin studies in the USA.

## Documents Checklist
The following documents are required for the Fulbright Scholarship application:
* Transcripts from all previous academic institutions
* Letters of recommendation from academic or professional references
* Personal statement or research proposal
* English language proficiency test scores (TOEFL or IELTS)
* Standardized test scores (GRE or GMAT)
* Copy of passport or national ID
* Proof of refugee or asylee status (if applicable)

## Frequently Asked Questions
1. What is the Fulbright Scholarship, and how can I apply?
The Fulbright Scholarship is a fully funded award that provides migrant students with the opportunity to pursue postgraduate studies in the USA. To apply, migrant students must meet the eligibility criteria and submit an online application form.
2. What are the eligibility criteria for the Fulbright Scholarship?
The eligibility criteria for the Fulbright Scholarship include having a bachelor's or master's degree, a minimum GPA of 3.0, and proficiency in English.
3. How much funding does the Fulbright Scholarship provide?
The Fulbright Scholarship provides up to $100,000 in funding per year for the duration of the study.
4. What is the deadline for applying to the Fulbright Scholarship?
The deadline for applying to the Fulbright Scholarship varies depending on the program, but it is typically between October and March of the following year.
5. Can I apply for the Fulbright Scholarship if I have a low GPA?
It is possible to apply for the Fulbright Scholarship with a low GPA, but it may be more difficult to be selected. Migrant students with a low GPA should highlight their strengths and achievements in other areas, such as work experience or volunteer work.
6. Do I need to take the TOEFL or IELTS to apply for the Fulbright Scholarship?
Yes, migrant students must take the TOEFL or IELTS to demonstrate their English language proficiency.
7. Can I apply for the Fulbright Scholarship if I am not a refugee or asylee?
Yes, migrant students who are not refugees or asylees can apply for the Fulbright Scholarship, but they must meet the eligibility criteria and have a valid visa.
8. How do I prepare for the Fulbright Scholarship interview?
Migrant students can prepare for the Fulbright Scholarship interview by researching the program and the institution they are applying to, practicing their English language skills, and preparing to discuss their academic and professional goals.

## External Resources
For more information on the Fulbright Scholarships, migrant students can visit the official website of the [US Department of State](https://www.state.gov/) or the [Institute of International Education](https://www.iie.org/).

## Conclusion
The Fulbright Scholarships are a great opportunity for migrant students to pursue postgraduate studies in the USA. With full funding and a wide range of programs to choose from, these scholarships can help migrant students achieve their academic and professional goals. To learn more about the Fulbright Scholarships and other opportunities for migrant students, please visit our [blog](https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog).

related.funding: Various scholarships offering full tuition, living costs, and other benefits to international students from Turkey
related.content:
## Introduction to USA Graduate Scholarships for International Students from Turkey
If you're a Turkish student looking to pursue a graduate degree in the USA, there are several country-specific scholarships available to help fund your education. These scholarships can cover full tuition, living costs, and other expenses, making it easier for you to achieve your academic goals. The USA is a popular destination for international students, and with the right scholarship, you can focus on your studies without financial worries.

## Who Qualifies for USA Graduate Scholarships
To be eligible for USA graduate scholarships, Turkish students typically need to hold a valid visa. The following visa categories are usually eligible:
* F-1 visa: For academic students
* J-1 visa: For exchange visitors
* M-1 visa: For vocational students
* Other visa categories may also be eligible, depending on the specific scholarship

## Available Scholarships
### Fulbright Graduate Scholarship
The Fulbright Graduate Scholarship offers $100,000 to cover tuition, living costs, and other expenses. To be eligible, applicants must hold a bachelor's degree, have a strong academic record, and demonstrate English proficiency. The deadline for applications is November 2026, and more information can be found on the official website: https://turkey.fulbrightonline.org/

### Turkish Coalition of America Scholarship
The Turkish Coalition of America Scholarship provides $5,000 to cover tuition and living costs. To be eligible, applicants must be Turkish students pursuing a graduate degree in the USA, have a strong academic record, and demonstrate financial need. The deadline for applications is March 2027, and more information can be found on the official website: https://www.tcacaf.org/scholarships

### ITI Turkish Scholarships
The ITI Turkish Scholarships offer $10,000 to cover tuition and living costs. To be eligible, applicants must be Turkish students pursuing a graduate degree in the USA, have a strong academic record, and demonstrate English proficiency. The deadline for applications is June 2027, and more information can be found on the official website: https://www.ititurkishscholarships.org/

### TUBITAK Research Fellowship
The TUBITAK Research Fellowship provides $20,000 to cover tuition, living costs, and research expenses. To be eligible, applicants must be Turkish students pursuing a graduate degree in the USA, have a strong academic record, and demonstrate research potential. The deadline for applications is September 2026, and more information can be found on the official website: https://www.tubitak.gov.tr/

### American-Turkish Society Scholarship
The American-Turkish Society Scholarship offers $5,000 to cover tuition and living costs. To be eligible, applicants must be Turkish students pursuing a graduate degree in the USA, have a strong academic record, and demonstrate financial need. The deadline for applications is January 2027, and more information can be found on the official website: https://www.american-turkishsociety.org/scholarships

## Application Steps
1. Research and choose a scholarship that aligns with your academic goals and eligibility criteria.
2. Review the application requirements and deadline for the chosen scholarship.
3. Prepare and submit the required documents, including transcripts, letters of recommendation, and a personal statement.
4. Take the required English proficiency exams, such as TOEFL or IELTS.
5. Submit the application form and supporting documents before the deadline.
6. Wait for the scholarship committee to review your application and make a decision.
7. If selected, receive the scholarship award and use it to cover tuition and living costs.
8. Maintain a strong academic record and meet the scholarship's requirements to renew the award.

## Documents Checklist
* Transcripts from previous academic institutions
* Letters of recommendation from academic or professional references
* Personal statement outlining academic goals and motivations
* English proficiency exam scores, such as TOEFL or IELTS
* Proof of financial need, such as bank statements or tax returns
* Copy of valid visa, such as F-1 or J-1
* Other documents required by the specific scholarship

## Frequently Asked Questions
1. What is the deadline for applying to USA graduate scholarships?
The deadline for applying to USA graduate scholarships varies depending on the specific scholarship, but it's usually between September and November 2026.
2. Can I apply for multiple scholarships at the same time?
Yes, you can apply for multiple scholarships at the same time, but make sure to review the eligibility criteria and application requirements for each scholarship.
3. What are the requirements for obtaining an F-1 visa?
To obtain an F-1 visa, you need to be accepted into a USA university, demonstrate English proficiency, and show proof of financial support.
4. How do I apply for a scholarship if I'm already in the USA on a different visa?
If you're already in the USA on a different visa, you can still apply for a scholarship, but you may need to meet additional requirements or obtain a new visa.
5. Can I use a scholarship to cover living costs, such as rent and food?
Yes, many scholarships cover living costs, such as rent and food, in addition to tuition fees.
6. How do I renew my scholarship award for the next academic year?
To renew your scholarship award, you need to maintain a strong academic record, meet the scholarship's requirements, and submit a renewal application before the deadline.
7. What are the tax implications of receiving a scholarship in the USA?
The tax implications of receiving a scholarship in the USA vary depending on your visa status and the type of scholarship, so it's recommended to consult with a tax professional.
8. Can I apply for a scholarship if I'm not fluent in English?
While English proficiency is often required for USA graduate scholarships, some scholarships may offer English language support or exempt applicants from English proficiency exams.

For more information on USA graduate scholarships, visit the official website of the US Department of State: https://educationusa.state.gov/ and the website of the Turkish Ministry of National Education: https://www.meb.gov.tr/

To learn more about scholarship opportunities and application tips, visit our blog at https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog

related.content:
## Fully Funded Graduate Scholarships for Refugee Students in the USA
If you're a refugee student looking to pursue graduate studies in the USA, you're likely wondering what fully funded scholarship options are available to you. The good news is that there are several organizations and institutions in the USA that offer fully funded graduate scholarships specifically for refugee students. These scholarships can cover the full cost of tuition, living expenses, and other related costs, making it possible for refugee students to pursue their graduate studies without financial burden.

## Who Qualifies
To be eligible for fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA, you must hold a specific visa category, including:
* F-1 visa
* F-2 visa
* J-1 visa
* J-2 visa
* Asylum status
* Refugee status
* Temporary Protected Status (TPS)
* Deferred Enforced Departure (DED)

## Available Scholarships
### The Paul and Daisy Soros Fellowships for New Americans
The Paul and Daisy Soros Fellowships for New Americans provides $100,000 over two years to support graduate studies. To be eligible, you must be a refugee or asylum seeker who has been admitted to a graduate program in the USA. The deadline to apply is November 1st. For more information, visit [https://www.pdsoros.org/](https://www.pdsoros.org/).
### The Fulbright Program
The Fulbright Program offers fully funded scholarships to international students, including refugees, to pursue graduate studies in the USA. The scholarship covers the full cost of tuition, living expenses, and other related costs. To be eligible, you must have a bachelor's degree and be admitted to a graduate program in the USA. The deadline to apply varies by country. For more information, visit [https://fulbright.americancouncils.org/](https://fulbright.americancouncils.org/).
### The Harvard University Refugee Scholarship
The Harvard University Refugee Scholarship provides full tuition and living expenses to refugee students pursuing graduate studies at Harvard University. To be eligible, you must be a refugee who has been admitted to a graduate program at Harvard University. The deadline to apply is December 15th. For more information, visit [https://www.hks.harvard.edu/](https://www.hks.harvard.edu/).
### The University of California, Berkeley Refugee Scholarship
The University of California, Berkeley Refugee Scholarship provides $10,000 to $20,000 per year to refugee students pursuing graduate studies at the University of California, Berkeley. To be eligible, you must be a refugee who has been admitted to a graduate program at the University of California, Berkeley. The deadline to apply is January 15th. For more information, visit [https://www.berkeley.edu/](https://www.berkeley.edu/).
### The New York University Refugee Scholarship
The New York University Refugee Scholarship provides full tuition and living expenses to refugee students pursuing graduate studies at New York University. To be eligible, you must be a refugee who has been admitted to a graduate program at New York University. The deadline to apply is February 15th. For more information, visit [https://www.nyu.edu/](https://www.nyu.edu/).

## Application Steps
1. Research the scholarship options available to you and review the eligibility criteria.
2. Review the application requirements and ensure you have all the necessary documents.
3. Submit your application, including all required documents, by the deadline.
4. Wait for the scholarship committee to review your application.
5. If selected, you will be notified and provided with instructions on how to accept the scholarship.
6. Apply for a visa, if required, and make travel arrangements to the USA.
7. Register for classes and attend orientation at your university.
8. Maintain good academic standing and comply with the terms of your scholarship.

## Documents Checklist
* Passport
* Visa
* Academic transcripts
* Letters of recommendation
* Personal statement
* Resume
* Proof of English proficiency (if required)
* Proof of financial support (if required)

## Frequently Asked Questions
What is the eligibility criteria for fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA?
To be eligible for fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA, you must hold a specific visa category, have been admitted to a graduate program in the USA, and meet the specific eligibility criteria for each scholarship.
How do I apply for fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA?
To apply for fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA, you must submit an application, including all required documents, by the deadline.
What is the deadline to apply for fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA?
The deadline to apply for fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA varies by scholarship, but is typically between November and February.
Can I apply for multiple fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA?
Yes, you can apply for multiple fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA, but you must meet the eligibility criteria for each scholarship.
How much funding is available for fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA?
The amount of funding available for fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA varies by scholarship, but can cover the full cost of tuition, living expenses, and other related costs.
What are the benefits of fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA?
The benefits of fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA include the opportunity to pursue graduate studies without financial burden, access to top universities and academic programs, and the opportunity to build a new life in the USA.
How do I maintain my visa status while pursuing graduate studies in the USA?
To maintain your visa status while pursuing graduate studies in the USA, you must comply with the terms of your visa and scholarship, and maintain good academic standing.
What are the requirements for English proficiency for fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA?
The requirements for English proficiency for fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA vary by university and scholarship, but typically require a minimum score on the TOEFL or IELTS exam.

For more information on fully funded graduate scholarships for refugee students in the USA, please visit the official website of the US Department of State at [https://www.state.gov/](https://www.state.gov/) and the official website of the US Department of Education at [https://www.ed.gov/](https://www.ed.gov/).
You can find more information and resources on scholarship opportunities on our blog at [https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog](https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog).

related.content:
## Introduction to USA's International Student Scholarship at the University of California for Asylum Seekers
The University of California offers various international student scholarships to asylum seekers pursuing undergraduate and graduate studies in the USA. These scholarships provide financial assistance to help asylum seekers achieve their academic goals. As an asylum seeker, you can apply for these scholarships to support your education in the United States.

## Who Qualifies
To be eligible for the University of California's international student scholarships, you must be an asylum seeker with a valid visa category, including:
* Asylum status (AS)
* Refugee status (RS)
* Temporary Protected Status (TPS)
* Deferred Enforced Departure (DED)
* Special Immigrant Visa (SIV)
* Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA)
* Withholding of Removal (WOR)
* Convention Against Torture (CAT)

## Available Scholarships
The University of California offers various scholarships to asylum seekers, including:
### The University of California President's Scholarship
The University of California President's Scholarship provides $10,000 to $20,000 per year to undergraduate and graduate students. To be eligible, you must be an incoming freshman or transfer student with a minimum GPA of 3.0. The deadline to apply is January 15th. For more information, visit [https://admission.universityofcalifornia.edu/](https://admission.universityofcalifornia.edu/).
### The UC Berkeley Scholarship
The UC Berkeley Scholarship provides $5,000 to $15,000 per year to undergraduate and graduate students. To be eligible, you must be a Berkeley student with a minimum GPA of 3.0. The deadline to apply is March 1st. For more information, visit [https://financialaid.berkeley.edu/](https://financialaid.berkeley.edu/).
### The UCLA Scholarship
The UCLA Scholarship provides $2,000 to $10,000 per year to undergraduate and graduate students. To be eligible, you must be a UCLA student with a minimum GPA of 3.0. The deadline to apply is April 1st. For more information, visit [https://financialaid.ucla.edu/](https://financialaid.ucla.edu/).
### The UC Davis Scholarship
The UC Davis Scholarship provides $1,500 to $8,000 per year to undergraduate and graduate students. To be eligible, you must be a UC Davis student with a minimum GPA of 3.0. The deadline to apply is May 1st. For more information, visit [https://financialaid.ucdavis.edu/](https://financialaid.ucdavis.edu/).
### The UC San Diego Scholarship
The UC San Diego Scholarship provides $1,000 to $6,000 per year to undergraduate and graduate students. To be eligible, you must be a UC San Diego student with a minimum GPA of 3.0. The deadline to apply is June 1st. For more information, visit [https://financialaid.ucsd.edu/](https://financialaid.ucsd.edu/).

## Application Steps
To apply for the University of California's international student scholarships, follow these steps:
1. Check your eligibility for the scholarship by reviewing the visa categories and GPA requirements.
2. Review the scholarship options and choose the ones that best fit your needs.
3. Gather all the required documents, including your academic transcripts and visa documents.
4. Submit your application through the university's online portal.
5. Pay the application fee, if required.
6. Upload your supporting documents, including your resume and personal statement.
7. Review and submit your application.
8. Check your email for updates on your application status.

## Documents Checklist
To apply for the University of California's international student scholarships, you will need to provide the following documents:
* Academic transcripts
* Visa documents (e.g. AS, RS, TPS, DED, SIV, DACA, WOR, CAT)
* Passport
* Resume
* Personal statement
* Letters of recommendation
* English language proficiency test scores (e.g. TOEFL, IELTS)

## Frequently Asked Questions
Q: What is the deadline to apply for the University of California's international student scholarships?
A: The deadline to apply varies depending on the scholarship and the university. Check the official website of the university for the most up-to-date information. You can also contact the university's financial aid office for more information.
Q: Do I need to have a valid visa to apply for the scholarship?
A: Yes, you must have a valid visa category, such as AS, RS, TPS, DED, SIV, DACA, WOR, or CAT, to be eligible for the scholarship. You can check your visa status on the US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) website.
Q: Can I apply for multiple scholarships?
A: Yes, you can apply for multiple scholarships, but you must meet the eligibility requirements for each scholarship. Make sure to review the requirements carefully before applying.
Q: How much money can I receive from the scholarship?
A: The amount of money you can receive from the scholarship varies depending on the scholarship and the university. Check the official website of the university for the most up-to-date information.
Q: Do I need to take an English language proficiency test to apply for the scholarship?
A: Yes, you may need to take an English language proficiency test, such as TOEFL or IELTS, to apply for the scholarship. Check the university's website for the most up-to-date information on English language proficiency requirements.
Q: Can I apply for the scholarship if I am already enrolled in a university in the USA?
A: Yes, you can apply for the scholarship if you are already enrolled in a university in the USA, but you must meet the eligibility requirements for the scholarship. Check the university's website for the most up-to-date information on eligibility requirements.
Q: How do I check the status of my application?
A: You can check the status of your application by logging into your account on the university's online portal. You can also contact the university's financial aid office for more information.
Q: What is the University of California's policy on asylum seekers?
A: The University of California is committed to providing a safe and inclusive environment for all students, including asylum seekers. Check the university's website for the most up-to-date information on policies and resources for asylum seekers.

## External Resources
For more information on the University of California's international student scholarships, visit the official website of the [University of California](https://www.universityofcalifornia.edu/). You can also visit the [US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS)](https://www.uscis.gov/) website for more information on visa categories and requirements.

## Closing
To learn more about scholarships and opportunities for asylum seekers in the USA, visit the [MigrantScholar blog](https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog) for the latest updates and information.
